lede: A look at May 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including José Mujica and Honoré de Balzac, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Russo-Ukrainian war
events.desc: Russo-Ukrainian war: Russia claims full control of the Ukrainian city of Mariupol after a nearly three-month siege.

events.title: Singapore
events.desc: The government of Singapore authorises the controversial execution of convicted murderer Kho Jabing for the murder of a Chinese construction worker despite international pleas for clemency, notably from Amnesty International and the United Nations.

events.title: 2012 Northern Italy earthquakes
events.desc: At least 27 people are killed and 50 others injured when a 6.0-magnitude earthquake strikes northern Italy.

events.title: Luttra Woman
events.desc: The Luttra Woman, a bog body from the Early Neolithic period (radiocarbon-dated c. 3928–3651 BC), is discovered near Luttra, Sweden.

events.title: Treaty of Jeddah (1927)
events.desc: Treaty of Jeddah: The United Kingdom recognizes the sovereignty of King Ibn Saud in the Kingdoms of Hejaz and Nejd, which later merge to become the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

events.title: Cuba
events.desc: Cuba gains independence from the United States. Tomás Estrada Palma becomes the country's first President.

events.title: Metre Convention
events.desc: Signing of the Metre Convention by 17 nations leading to the establishment of the International System of Units.

events.title: Abraham Lincoln
events.desc: U.S. President Abraham Lincoln signs the Homestead Act into law, opening eighty-four million acres (340,000 km2) of public land to settlers.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: Johann Sebastian Bach leads the first performance of his cantata for Pentecost, Erschallet, ihr Lieder, BWV 172, at the chapel of Schloss Weimar.

events.title: First Council of Nicaea
events.desc: The First Council of Nicaea is formally opened, starting the first ecumenical council of the Christian Church.

births.name: José Mujica
births.line: Uruguayan guerrilla leader and politician, 40th President of Uruguay

births.name: David Paterson
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 55th Governor of New York

births.name: Eduard Buchner
births.line: German chemist, zymologist, and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: John Stuart Mill
births.line: English economist, civil servant, and philosopher

deaths.text: Damaskinos of Athens, Greek archbishop and politician, 137th Prime Minister of Greece
Philipp Lenard, Slovak-German physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Władysław IV Vasa, Polish son of Sigismund III Vasa
Christopher Columbus, Italian explorer, early European explorer of the Americas
